mission.title: Install and Configure VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid
mission.description: VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid is a distribution that provides a comprehensive platform for deploying and managing Kubernetes clusters in an enterprise environment. It leverages the Tanzu Framework and Carvel packaging to enhance the user experience and integration. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with VMware tools.

steps.title: Install the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Tanzu CLI, which is essential for managing Tanzu Kubernetes Grid.

```bash

curl -L https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/tanzu-framework/releases/download/v0.29.0/tanzu-framework-linux-amd64.tar.gz | tar xz -C /usr/local/bin

```

> Install the Tanzu CLI binary

steps.title: Initialize the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step initializes the Tanzu CLI and sets up the necessary environment.

```bash

tanzu init

```

> Initializes the Tanzu CLI environment

steps.title: Create a Tanzu Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Tanzu Kubernetes cluster with specified Kubernetes version and node count.

```bash

tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --node-count 3 --region us-west-2

```

> Creates a cluster named 'my-cluster' with 3 nodes
commands: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --node-count 3 --region us-west-2

steps.title: Configure kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures the kubeconfig file to access the newly created cluster.

```bash

tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context

```

> Sets the kubeconfig context for the cluster

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step verifies that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Lists all nodes in the cluster

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Tanzu Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Tanzu Kubernetes cluster and may result in data loss. Ensure to backup any important data before proceeding.

```bash

tanzu cluster delete my-cluster

```

> Deletes the cluster named 'my-cluster'

uninstall.title: Remove Tanzu CLI
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Tanzu CLI from your system.

```bash

rm /usr/local/bin/tanzu

```

> Removes the Tanzu CLI binary

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Tanzu Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to backup your cluster data. This step upgrades the cluster to a new version.

```bash

tanzu cluster upgrade my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.29.0

```

> Upgrades the cluster to Kubernetes version 1.29.0

upgrade.title: Verify Upgrade
upgrade.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes after the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Verifies that all nodes are in a Ready state

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified node count exceeds available resources in the selected region.

**Fix:**
Reduce the node count or select a different region with sufficient resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubectl command-line tool is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl by following the installation instructions from the Kubernetes website.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig context for the cluster is not set correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context' to set the context.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster is still initializing or there are issues with the nodes.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the nodes with 'kubectl get nodes' and ensure they are all in Ready state.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.29, improved integration with Carvel packaging.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.27.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.4, ensure to backup your cluster and follow the upgrade instructions.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in the Ready state.
codeSnippets: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --node-count 3 --region us-west-2

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
